name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Round 4 refines the v3 minimalism direction: cradle shelf widens 108→110 so the tray-holder side walls match the 3 mm wall_thickness rule, the tray gets a continuous r=2 top-edge fillet around the entire perimeter, and the front-wall scoop is rebuilt as a single smooth concave arc (r=18) from the floor's front edge up to a recessed top lip — replacing the round-3 45° scoop + central finger-grip. Quiet desk dock — function and frame, nothing more.

params.cable_slot_style: U-notch in -Y wall, z=0..20mm. Wall above notch (z=20..25, 5mm × 25mm) prints as a short bridge between the side walls of the notch — well within FDM bridging tolerance.
params.render_with_host_note: Top-level OpenSCAD parameter render_with_host (default false). When true, host_object_proxy() draws a 78x152x143 box at the printer's installed position inside the pocket. STL exports MUST keep render_with_host=false (proxy excluded from print geometry); use-state PNG renders override via --param render_with_host=true.
params.fillet_schedule_note: Two tiers + two function-driven exceptions. r=3 utility on top edges of cradle perimeter walls, all break-edges. r=10 hero on cradle exterior corners (4x), base plate corners (4x top + 4x bottom), printer-section→shelf concave fillet on both ±X sides. r=1.5 on foot-to-plate upper blend (foot bottom stays flat for first-layer adhesion). r=2 continuous fillet on the entire tray top edge (round-4 documented exception — the smaller radius reads better at the tray's smaller scale). NO chamfers anywhere. NO mid-tier radii.
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (assumed, not confirmed in product photos)
params.tray_scoop_note: Round 4 replaces the 45° scoop + central finger-grip with a single smooth concave circular arc on the front (+Y) wall. Arc start: floor's front edge (y=ext_d=94.2, z=floor_t=1.6). Arc end: recessed top lip (y=ext_d-12=82.2, z=ext_h=21.6). Arc radius 18 mm. The exterior and interior arcs are offset by wall_t=1.6 so the curved front wall has uniform 1.6 mm wall thickness. The user grabs the recessed top lip (soft-rounded by the r=2 top-edge fillet) for tray removal — no separate finger-grip needed.
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here, printer faces user). -Y = user-back (against-wall, cable notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views. PRIMARY hero is the use-state render with the printer proxy installed — the 'is the cradle quiet next to the printer?' check runs on cradle-user-front-in-use.png.
params.print_orientation_cradle: Base down, walls vertical (functional/use orientation). No supports needed — all features under 45° overhang.
params.print_orientation_tray: Back face on bed (-Y down), open top facing +Y in print-frame. The tray is modeled in functional (use) orientation in tray.scad; the slicer should rotate it 90° about +X so the back wall sits on the build plate. This orients the concave scoop's near-horizontal top tangent as an upward-facing slope, eliminating the severe overhang that would result from printing open-top-up. With this orientation no supports are needed; seam lands on the +Y curved front face (acceptable since the curve is the user-front feature, but seam alignment can be controlled in the slicer).

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 21.6 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle.

Front wall is a single smooth concave circular arc (r=18) from floor to recessed top lip. The arc's near-horizontal tangent at the top is a severe overhang in the open-top-up orientation; mitigated by printing tray on its back (-Y down on bed). Verify slicer's print-on-back orientation produces clean upward-facing slope on the scoop, no supports added, and the visible front face has acceptable seam placement.

Round-4 addition: continuous quarter-arc roll around the entire tray top edge (back, sides, scoop top lip). Verify the fillet renders smoothly without facet stepping and reads as a soft top edge in user-front-threequarter views.

Wall above the cable notch (z=20..25, 5mm tall × 25mm wide) prints as a short bridge between the notch's side walls. 25mm span is within FDM bridging tolerance for PLA, but verify slicer does not add unnecessary supports and the bridge surface is clean.
Generous corner radius on a 25mm low wall — confirm it reads as 'quiet soft footprint' rather than 'bulbous' or 'cartoony'. Print-reviewer aesthetic check.

Quarter-arc concave sweep on ±X sides of the cradle at the printer-section→shelf transition. Hero radius bumped from r=8 to r=10 in v3. Verify the fillet reads as a deliberate sculptural move (the one sculptural move on the cradle) rather than a slicer artifact.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity.
